buildTheseSix.coverage: Normal tanks: Blissey, Chansey, Snorlax, Slaking

Magnemite Candy is accessible and Steel typing cuts potion use.
Litwick events make Candy obtainable; this moveset needs no Elite TM.
buildTheseSix.coverage: Psychic/Ghost; fast move still pressures Steel

buildTheseSix.budgetReason: Mudkip Candy is common from events and Swampert is sturdy.
buildTheseSix.build: Level 35–40; Earthquake avoids an Elite TM

defenders.motivationNote: High CP decays faster than low-CP specialists.
defenders.placementValue: Maximum time wall; best early to create berry reaction time.
defenders.whyLine: Normal typing, 169 Def / 496 Sta — weak only to Fighting, resists 1.

defenders.motivationNote: Lower CP preserves motivation longer than Blissey.
defenders.placementValue: Excellent low-decay bulk and long-term motivation.
defenders.whyLine: Normal typing, 128 Def / 487 Sta — weak only to Fighting, resists 1.

defenders.motivationNote: Moderate decay; broad charged coverage can cost attacker healing.
defenders.placementValue: Bulky neutral defender, but do not place next to another Normal tank.
defenders.whyLine: Normal typing, 169 Def / 330 Sta — weak only to Fighting, resists 1.

defenders.placementValue: Large CP is visually imposing, but Yawn deals negligible fast-move pressure and Fighting sweeps it.

defenders.placementValue: Punishes Fighting and breaks Normal-tank chains; weak to common Ground, Fire, Ghost, and Dark counters.
defenders.whyLine: Steel/Psychic typing, 228 Def / 190 Sta — weak to 4 types, resists 10.

defenders.motivationNote: Useful weakness break despite moderate decay.
defenders.placementValue: Direct Fighting punishment between Normal tanks.
defenders.whyLine: Fairy/Flying typing, 217 Def / 198 Sta — weak to 5 types, resists 6.

defenders.motivationNote: High CP decay; place for immediate coverage.
defenders.placementValue: Resists Fighting and Ground, forcing an Ice or Fairy answer.

defenders.placementValue: Fighting punisher with less bulk than Togekiss but heavy Charm pressure.

defenders.motivationNote: Good bulk without extreme CP decay.

defenders.placementValue: Low-CP bulk preserves motivation and slows casual attackers.

gym.methodology: Practical delay, weakness diversity, solo reliability, healing cost, and low-resource accessibility; gym defense can delay but cannot guarantee a hold.

placementWarnings.message: Do not follow Slaking with Snorlax (or Blissey/Chansey): one Fighting team can sweep the chain.
placementWarnings.recommendation: Insert a fighter punisher such as Togekiss, Gardevoir, Drifblim, Dragonite, or Metagross before another Normal tank.

soloOffense.advice: Use a super-effective same-type fast/charged set and keep one bulky neutral backup.
soloOffense.title: Match the defender, not the auto-team

soloOffense.advice: After a win, exit instead of continuing down the lineup, then re-enter the first defender to reduce its motivation again.
soloOffense.title: Clear one defender at a time when berries are likely

soloOffense.advice: Dodge slow charged moves on bulky defenders; favor Machamp, Excadrill, and Swampert when glassier counters would consume revives.
soloOffense.title: Protect healing supplies

soloOffense.advice: Level 35–40 weather-boosted budget counters with the right moves are usually enough for solo gym work; save XL for higher-value raid or Master League projects.
soloOffense.title: Do not overbuild

staggerGuide.caveat: Exact spacing is not guaranteed: network lag, lobby grouping, defender Golden Razz Berries, battle errors, and live game changes can merge or invalidate waves. Treat this as coordination guidance, not a promised knockout timer.
staggerGuide.goal: Create separate battle waves against the first defender so its motivation can fall in quick succession and the pair avoids wasting time on the full lineup.

steps.action: Preselect the correct counter team, confirm the first defender, and agree that Player A starts alone. Do not tap Battle together.
Enter and begin the first defender. Call out when the GO screen has cleared and damage is registering.
Enter after A's battle is established so the game is more likely to create a later battle wave. Use the defender HP/state on your own screen, not a rigid stopwatch.
After the win registers, use the exit/back option instead of continuing to defender two, then re-enter the first slot for the next motivation drop.
Finish your wave and also exit rather than advancing. Call the result so A knows whether the defender was berried or removed.
Repeat on the same first defender until removed, then reassess the new first slot. If both players merge into one battle, separate and restart the spacing rather than assuming the stagger worked.

staggerGuide.title: Two-player focused stagger
